name: Mayeritsa (Greek Easter Soup)
recipeIngredient: Entrails (liver, heart) of a very young spring lamb.
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the entrails thoroughly in plenty of water. Scald them in boiling water for about 2 minutes, then cool and cut into small pieces.
2. Melt butter in a pot over medium heat. Sauté the onion until light golden brown, adding scallion and chopped meat several times, turning the meat as needed.
3. Add dill, salt, and pepper to taste, along with enough broth to cover the ingredients. Bring to a boil and simmer covered for 1½ to 3 hours, or until the meat is tender.
4. About 20 minutes before the soup is done, add the raw rice and additional broth if necessary. Stir gently to combine.
5. In a separate bowl, beat the eggs and stir in lemon juice thoroughly.
6. Gradually add some of the hot liquid from the pot into the beaten egg mixture, whisking constantly to avoid scrambling the eggs.
7. Pour the egg-lemon mixture back into the soup, stirring well to incorporate.
8. Serve hot.
NOTE: The broth is typically made from parts of the lamb such as head, breast, or shoulder; however, any meat stock can be used.
